tools.name: mcp__claude_ai_Gmail__gmail_search_messages
tools.description:
Searches Gmail messages using powerful query syntax with support for filtering by sender, recipient, subject, labels, dates, and more.

This tool provides access to Gmail's full search capabilities. Returns structured data with citation metadata for proper attribution.

GMAIL SEARCH SYNTAX:
- from:sender@example.com - Messages from specific sender
- to:recipient@example.com - Messages to specific recipient
- subject:meeting - Messages with "meeting" in subject
- is:unread - Unread messages
- is:starred - Starred messages
- label:important - Messages with specific label
- has:attachment - Messages with attachments
- after:2024/1/1 before:2024/12/31 - Date range
- "exact phrase" - Exact phrase match
- OR operator: from:alice@example.com OR from:bob@example.com
- Exclude with minus: -from:spam@example.com
- Combine multiple: is:unread from:boss@company.com has:attachment

PAGINATION: Results are limited per request. Use pagination for complete results:
1. First call returns messages and may include nextPageToken
2. Call again with pageToken parameter to get next batch
3. Continue until no nextPageToken is returned or sufficient results are found

Args:
    q (Optional[str]): Gmail search query using standard Gmail search syntax. If omitted, returns most recent messages
    pageToken (Optional[str]): Token from previous response to retrieve next page of results
    maxResults (Optional[int]): Maximum messages to return per request (1-500). Default: 20
    includeSpamTrash (Optional[bool]): Include messages from SPAM and TRASH folders. Default: false

Returns structured data with citation metadata for proper attribution.

q.description: Query string using Gmail search syntax. Examples: "from:user@example.com", "is:unread", "subject:meeting"

pageToken.description: Page token to retrieve a specific page of results

maxResults.description: Maximum number of messages to return (max: 500)

includeSpamTrash.description: Include messages from SPAM and TRASH

tools.name: mcp__claude_ai_Google_Calendar__gcal_create_event
tools.description:
Creates a new event on a Google Calendar with comprehensive details including attendees, reminders, and recurrence rules.

This tool creates calendar events with full customization options. The event organizer is automatically set to the authenticated account. Note: explicitly add organizer email to the attendees array.

Conference rooms and resources can be booked by adding them as attendees with resource: true. First use gcal_list_calendars to find available resource calendars (they have IDs ending in @resource.calendar.google.com), then check their availability with gcal_find_meeting_times before booking.

Args:
    calendarId (str): The calendar ID where the event will be created. Default: 'primary' (user's main calendar)
    event (object): Event details object with the following structure:
        - summary (str, required): Event title/name
        - description (Optional[str]): Detailed event description
        - location (Optional[str]): Event location (physical address or meeting link)
        - start (object, required): Event start time with one of:
            - dateTime (str): Start timestamp in RFC3339 format (e.g., 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' for UTC, 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS-07:00' for PDT)
            - date (str): For all-day events in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            - timeZone (Optional[str]): IANA timezone (e.g., 'America/Los_Angeles')
        - end (object, required): Event end time with same format as start
        - attendees (Optional[array]): List of attendees including people and resources. Each attendee should have:
            - email (str): Attendee's email address (for conference rooms, use their @resource.calendar.google.com email)
            - displayName (Optional[str]): Attendee's display name
            - optional (Optional[bool]): Whether attendance is optional
            - organizer (Optional[bool]): Set to true to indicate this attendee is the organizer
        - recurrence (Optional[array[str]]): RRULE strings for recurring events (e.g., ['RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAвА¶ [truncated]

calendarId.description: The calendar ID to create the event in

dateTime.description: Start time (RFC3339 timestamp with timezone, e.g., YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ)
End time (RFC3339 timestamp with timezone, e.g., YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ)

email.description: Attendee email (for resources like rooms, use their @resource.calendar.google.com email)

organizer.description: Set to true if this attendee is the organizer

attendees.description: List of attendees including people and resources (conference rooms). Include the organizer's email here if you want them to appear in the attendees list

type.description: Conference solution type (e.g., 'hangoutsMeet' for Google Meet)

colorId.description: Event color ID (string '1'-'11'): 1=Lavender, 2=Sage, 3=Grape, 4=Flamingo, 5=Banana, 6=Tangerine, 7=Peacock, 8=Graphite, 9=Blueberry, 10=Basil, 11=Tomato. In Google Calendar, event colors function as categories вАФ settable per-event or per-series. Users may assign custom labels to colors in the web UI (e.g., '1:1s', 'Break'), but the API only exposes numeric IDs, not those labels. Only affects your own calendar view вАФ each attendee controls their own event color.

tools.name: mcp__claude_ai_Google_Calendar__gcal_delete_event
tools.description:
Permanently deletes a calendar event with automatic attendee notification.

This tool removes an event from Google Calendar. If you're the organizer, all attendees will receive cancellation notifications. This action is irreversible - the event cannot be recovered once deleted.

Args:
    calendarId (str, required): The calendar containing the event (e.g., 'primary' or specific calendar ID)
    eventId (str, required): The unique ID of the event to delete (obtained from gcal_list_events or gcal_get_event)

Returns:
    str: Confirmation message indicating:
    - Event successfully deleted
    - Event ID for reference
    - Whether cancellation notices were sent to attendees

Examples:
    - Use when: "Cancel meeting abc123def" -> gcal_delete_event(calendarId="primary", eventId="abc123def")
    - Use when: "Remove the event xyz789 from my work calendar" -> gcal_delete_event(calendarId="work@company.com", eventId="xyz789")
    - Use when: "Delete the duplicate appointment" -> First find the duplicate with gcal_list_events, then delete it
    - Don't use when: You want to decline an invitation (use gcal_respond_to_event with 'declined' instead)
    - Don't use when: You want to hide an event but keep the record (no hiding feature - consider updating description to mark as cancelled)

Important Notes:
    - Deleting a recurring event deletes ALL occurrences
    - If you're the organizer, attendees receive cancellation emails automatically
    - If you're not the organizer, only your copy is removed (event remains for others)
    - Deleted events cannot be restored - consider updating the event instead if unsure

Error Handling:
    - Returns error if event doesn't exist or was already deleted
    - Returns error if you don't have permission to delete the event
    - Handles calendar access errors gracefully
    - Provides clear message if attempting to delete events from read-only calendars

calendarId.description: The ID of the calendar containing the event

eventId.description: The ID of the event to delete

tools.name: mcp__claude_ai_Google_Calendar__gcal_find_meeting_times
tools.description:
Finds optimal meeting times when all specified attendees are available by checking their calendar availability.

This tool uses Google's FreeBusy API to efficiently check multiple calendars simultaneously and identify time slots where all attendees can meet. The authenticated user's calendar is automatically included in the availability check. Results respect business hours and exclude weekends by default, but these preferences can be customized.

Args:
    attendees (array[str], required): List of email addresses to check availability for, including conference room emails (@resource.calendar.google.com). The authenticated user is automatically included
    duration (int, required): Required meeting duration in minutes (e.g., 30, 60, 90)
    timeMin (str, required): Start of search range (RFC3339 timestamp without timezone, e.g., 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS')
    timeMax (str, required): End of search range (RFC3339 timestamp without timezone, e.g., 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS'). Must be after timeMin
    timeZone (Optional[str]): IANA timezone that will be used to parse timeMin and timeMax and for displaying results (e.g., 'America/Los_Angeles')
    preferences (Optional[object]): Scheduling preferences:
        - startHour (Optional[int]): Earliest hour to start meetings (0-23). Default: 9
        - endHour (Optional[int]): Latest hour to end meetings (0-23). Default: 17
        - excludeWeekends (Optional[bool]): Skip Saturday/Sunday. Default: true
        - maxResults (Optional[int]): Maximum slots to return. Default: 5

Returns:
    str: Available time slots formatted as:

    === Available Meeting Times (60 minutes) ===

    Option 1: Monday, Apr 6, 2026
    вП∞ 10:00 AM - 11:00 AM (PDT)
    вЬЕ All 4 attendees available

    Option 2: Monday, Apr 6, 2026
    вП∞ 2:00 PM - 3:00 PM (PDT)
    вЬЕ All 4 attendees available

    Option 3: Apr 4, 2026
    вП∞ 9:00 AM - 10:00 AM (PDT)
    вЬЕ All 4 attendees available

    --- SEARCH SUMMARY ---
    Checked availability for: alice@example.com, bob@example.com, carol@example.com
    Time rвА¶ [truncated]

attendees.description: Email addresses of people to check availability for

timeMin.description: Start of time range to search (RFC3339 timestamp without timezone, e.g., YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS)

timeMax.description: End of time range to search (RFC3339 timestamp without timezone, e.g., YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS)

timeZone.description: Time zone that will be used to parse timeMin and timeMax and for the results (IANA Time Zone Database name)

maxResults.description: Maximum number of slots to return

tools.name: mcp__claude_ai_Google_Calendar__gcal_find_my_free_time
tools.description:
Identifies free time slots in your personal calendar(s) where no events are scheduled.

This tool analyzes your calendar(s) to find gaps between events, helping you identify available time for focused work, personal tasks, or new meetings. Only checks calendars you specify - does not check other people's availability.

Args:
    calendarIds (array[str], required): List of your calendar IDs to check (e.g., ['primary', 'work@company.com'])
    timeMin (str, required): Start of range to check (RFC3339 timestamp without timezone, e.g., 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS')
    timeMax (str, required): End of range to check (RFC3339 timestamp without timezone, e.g., 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS'). Must be after timeMin
    timeZone (Optional[str]): IANA timezone that will be used to parse timeMin and timeMax and for displaying results (e.g., 'America/New_York')
    minDuration (Optional[int]): Minimum free slot duration in minutes to include. Default: 30

Returns:
    JSON object containing:
    - timeRange: Object with start, end, and timeZone fields
    - freeSlots: Array of free time slots, each containing:
      - start: ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone
      - end: ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone
      - startFormatted: Human-readable start time
      - endFormatted: Human-readable end time
      - duration: Duration in minutes as a string
    - totalFreeSlots: Number of free time slots found
    - summary: Brief description of results

Examples:
    - Use when: "Find my free time this week" -> gcal_find_my_free_time(calendarIds=["primary"], timeMin="2026-04-06T00:00:00", timeMax="2026-04-10T23:59:59", timeZone="America/New_York")
    - Use when: "When do I have 2 hours free for deep work?" -> gcal_find_my_free_time(calendarIds=["primary"], timeMin="2026-04-06T00:00:00", timeMax="2026-04-10T23:59:59", minDuration=120)
    - Use when: "Show me gaps in my schedule tomorrow" -> gcal_find_my_free_time(calendarIds=["primary"], timeMin="2026-04-04T00:00:00", timeMax="2026-04-04T23:59:59")
    - Don't use when: Finding mutual availability wiвА¶ [truncated]

calendarIds.description: List of your calendar IDs to check for availability

timeMin.description: Start of time range to check (RFC3339 timestamp without timezone, e.g., YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS)

timeMax.description: End of time range to check (RFC3339 timestamp without timezone, e.g., YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS)

minDuration.description: Minimum duration of free slots in minutes

tools.name: mcp__claude_ai_Google_Calendar__gcal_get_event
tools.description:
Retrieves complete details about a specific calendar event.

This tool fetches comprehensive information about a single event using its unique ID. Useful for viewing full event details, checking attendee responses, or gathering information before making updates.

Args:
    calendarId (str, required): The calendar containing the event (e.g., 'primary' or specific calendar ID)
    eventId (str, required): The unique ID of the event (obtained from gcal_list_events or event creation)

Returns:
    str: JSON string of the full event details
    JSON fields:
      - id
      - summary
      - description
      - location
      - start: { date, dateTime, timeZone }
      - end: { date, dateTime, timeZone }
      - allDay
      - status
      - myResponseStatus
      - hasAttachments
      - htmlLink
      - creator: { displayName, email, id, self }
      - organizer: { displayName, email, id, self }
      - numAttendees
      - recurrence
      - recurringEventId
      - visibility
      - transparency
      - attachments: [{ fileUrl, title, fileId }]
      - created
      - updated
      - attendees: [{ email, displayName, responseStatus, comment, optional, additionalGuests, organizer, self }]

Examples:
    - Use when: "Show me details for event abc123def" -> gcal_get_event(calendarId="primary", eventId="abc123def")
    - Use when: "Check who has accepted the meeting xyz789" -> gcal_get_event(calendarId="primary", eventId="xyz789")
    - Use when: "What's the video link for event meet123?" -> gcal_get_event(calendarId="primary", eventId="meet123")
    - Use when: Preparing to update an event and need current details first
    - Don't use when: Looking for multiple events (use gcal_list_events instead)
    - Don't use when: You need to check availability (use gcal_find_meeting_times instead)

Error Handling:
    - Returns error if event doesn't exist or was deleted
    - Returns error if you don't have permission to view the event
    - Handles missing optional fields gracefully (e.g., events without descriptions or loвА¶ [truncated]

calendarId.description: The ID of the calendar containing the event

eventId.description: The ID of the event to retrieve

tools.name: mcp__claude_ai_Google_Calendar__gcal_list_calendars
tools.description:
Lists calendars that have been added to your Google Calendar sidebar/list.

IMPORTANT: This only shows calendars you've explicitly subscribed to or that appear in your calendar list. It does NOT show all calendars you have permission to access. For example, a coworker's calendar (john@company.com) won't appear here unless you've added it to your calendar list, but you can still view their events directly using gcal_list_events(calendarId="john@company.com") if they've shared it with you.

PAGINATION: If you have access to many calendars, results will be paginated:
1. First call returns calendars and may include "Next page token: xyz789"
2. Call again with pageToken="xyz789" to get additional calendars
3. Continue until no page token is returned to ensure you see all accessible calendars

Args:
    pageToken (Optional[str]): Token for pagination. When response shows "Next page token: xxx", use that token here to retrieve additional calendars

Returns:
    JSON object with calendar list containing:
    - calendars: Array of calendar objects, each with:
      - id: Calendar identifier (email for user calendars, resource ID for rooms)
      - summary: Display name of the calendar
      - primary: Boolean indicating if this is the user's primary calendar
      - accessRole: Permission level (owner, writer, reader, freeBusyReader)
      - backgroundColor: Hex color code for calendar background
      - foregroundColor: Hex color code for calendar text
      - colorId: Google Calendar color ID
      - timeZone: Calendar's time zone
      - selected: Whether calendar is selected in UI
      - isResource: Boolean indicating if this is a resource calendar (room, equipment)
      - description: Optional calendar description
      - location: Optional location for resource calendars
      - summaryOverride: Optional custom display name
      - defaultReminders: Array of default reminder settings
      - notificationSettings: Email notification preferences
      - conferenceProperties: Allowed conference solution types
    - nвА¶ [truncated]

pageToken.description: Token for pagination. Use the nextPageToken from previous response.

tools.name: mcp__claude_ai_Google_Calendar__gcal_list_events
tools.description:
Lists calendar events within a specified time range with powerful filtering and search capabilities.

This tool retrieves events from Google Calendar with options to filter by time, search terms, and pagination. Events are returned in chronological order with all relevant details. Recurring events are automatically expanded into individual occurrences.

CALENDAR ACCESS: You can view events from ANY calendar you have permission to access by using their email/ID directly - the calendar does NOT need to be in your calendar list. For example, if a colleague shares their calendar with you, use calendarId="colleague@company.com" even if it doesn't appear in gcal_list_calendars.

PAGINATION: When there are more events than maxResults, the response will include a "nextPageToken". To get all events:
1. First call returns 50 events and nextPageToken
2. Call again with pageToken parameter to get the next 50 events
3. Continue until no page token is returned
This is essential for getting complete results when querying busy calendars or long time ranges.

Args:
    calendarId (str): The calendar to query. Default: 'primary' (your main calendar). Can be an email address (e.g., 'colleague@company.com') to view someone else's calendar if they've shared it with you. Note: The calendar doesn't need to be in your calendar list - you can access any calendar you have permission to view
    q (Optional[str]): Free text search query to find events containing specific terms (searches in summary, description, location, attendee names/emails)
    timeMin (Optional[str]): Lower bound for event's end time in RFC3339 format (e.g., '2026-04-03T09:00:00'). This time MUST be in the user's local timezone. Events ending before this time are excluded
    timeMax (Optional[str]): Upper bound for event's start time in RFC3339 format (e.g., '2026-04-03T17:00:00'). This time MUST be in the user's local timezone. Events starting after this time are excluded
    timeZone (Optional[str]): IANA timezone for interpreting times in the request and response (вА¶ [truncated]

calendarId.description: The calendar ID. Use 'primary' for the user's main calendar.

q.description: Free text search terms to find events

timeMin.description: Lower bound for event's end time (RFC3339 timestamp without timezone, e.g., YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS).

timeMax.description: Upper bound for event's start time (RFC3339 timestamp without timezone, e.g., YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS).

timeZone.description: Time zone that will be used to parse timeMin and timeMin and used in the response (IANA Time Zone Database name)

condenseEventDetails.description: If true only a subset of event details will be returned to minimize response size. Very helpful for long time range queries

maxResults.description: Maximum number of events to return (max: 250)

tools.name: mcp__claude_ai_Google_Calendar__gcal_respond_to_event
tools.description:
Responds to calendar invitations with your attendance decision and optional message to the organizer.

This tool updates your RSVP status for events you've been invited to. Your response is immediately reflected in the event and the organizer receives a notification with your decision and any included message.

Args:
    calendarId (str): The calendar containing the event. Default: 'primary' (your main calendar)
    eventId (str, required): The unique ID of the event to respond to (obtained from gcal_list_events)
    response (str, required): Your attendance decision - must be one of:
        - 'accepted': You will attend the event
        - 'declined': You will not attend the event
        - 'tentative': You might attend (maybe)
    comment (Optional[str]): Message to send to the organizer with your response (e.g., "Looking forward to it!" or "Sorry, I have a conflict")
    sendUpdates (Optional[str]): Who receives notification of your response. Default: 'all'
        - 'all': Notify all attendees
        - 'externalOnly': Only notify attendees outside your domain
        - 'none': Don't send notifications

Returns:
    str: Confirmation showing:
    - Your response status (accepted/declined/tentative)
    - Comment sent to organizer (if any)
    - Updated event details
    - Other attendees' response status

Examples:
    - Use when: "Accept the team meeting invitation" -> gcal_respond_to_event(eventId="meet123", response="accepted")
    - Use when: "Decline event xyz789 with a message saying I have a conflict" -> gcal_respond_to_event(eventId="xyz789", response="declined", comment="Sorry, I have a conflict at this time")
    - Use when: "Mark myself as tentative for the Friday social" -> gcal_respond_to_event(eventId="social456", response="tentative", comment="I'll try to make it!")
    - Use when: "Accept the interview but don't notify others" -> gcal_respond_to_event(eventId="interview789", response="accepted", sendUpdates="none")
    - Don't use when: You're the organizer (you're automatically attending youвА¶ [truncated]

calendarId.description: The ID of the calendar containing the event

eventId.description: The ID of the event to respond to

comment.description: Optional comment to send with your response

tools.name: mcp__claude_ai_Google_Calendar__gcal_update_event
tools.description:
Updates an existing calendar event with new information while preserving unchanged fields.

This tool modifies existing events on Google Calendar. Only include the fields you want to change - all other fields remain unchanged. When updating attendees, you must provide the complete list (both existing attendees you want to keep and new ones to add).

Args:
    calendarId (str, required): The calendar containing the event (e.g., 'primary' or specific calendar ID)
    eventId (str, required): The unique ID of the event to update (obtained from gcal_list_events or gcal_create_event)
    event (object): Fields to update (only include what you want to change):
        - summary (Optional[str]): New event title/name
        - description (Optional[str]): New event description
        - location (Optional[str]): New location (physical address or meeting link)
        - start (Optional[object]): New start time with:
            - dateTime (Optional[str]): Timestamp in RFC3339 format (e.g., 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ')
            - date (Optional[str]): For all-day events (YYYY-MM-DD)
            - timeZone (Optional[str]): IANA timezone
        - end (Optional[object]): New end time (same format as start)
        - attendees (Optional[array]): COMPLETE list of attendees including people and resources (include both existing and new):
            - email (str): Attendee's email (for conference rooms, use @resource.calendar.google.com email)
            - displayName (Optional[str]): Display name
            - optional (Optional[bool]): Whether attendance is optional
            - organizer (Optional[bool]): Set to true to indicate this attendee is the organizer
        - conferenceData (Optional[object]): Conference/video call settings:
            - createRequest (object): Request to create a new conference:
                - conferenceSolutionKey (object):
                    - type (str): Conference type ('hangoutsMeet' for Google Meet)
                - requestId (str): Unique ID for this request (use any unique string)
   вА¶ [truncated]

calendarId.description: The ID of the calendar containing the event

eventId.description: The ID of the event to update

dateTime.description: Start time (RFC3339 timestamp with timezone, e.g., YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ)
End time (RFC3339 timestamp with timezone, e.g., YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ)

type.description: Conference solution type (e.g., 'hangoutsMeet' for Google Meet)

colorId.description: Event color ID (string '1'-'11'): 1=Lavender, 2=Sage, 3=Grape, 4=Flamingo, 5=Banana, 6=Tangerine, 7=Peacock, 8=Graphite, 9=Blueberry, 10=Basil, 11=Tomato. In Google Calendar, event colors function as categories вАФ settable per-event or per-series. Users may assign custom labels to colors in the web UI (e.g., '1:1s', 'Break'), but the API only exposes numeric IDs, not those labels. Only affects your own calendar view вАФ each attendee controls their own event color.
